claim: Claude Sonnet 4.5 is state-of-the-art on SWEBench Verified (92.3% vs GPT-5 at ~73%), Terminal Bench (50%), and computer use benchmarks
actionable: Use Claude Sonnet 4.5 as your primary coding model; benchmark your current setup against SWEBench scores to validate improvement

claim: Claude Sonnet 4.5 can autonomously think for 30+ hours independently; task duration capability is doubling every 7 months (new scaling law replacing Moore's Law)
actionable: Plan for exponential growth in long-horizon task capability; design agents to leverage extended thinking time

claim: Anthropic achieved 30-hour thinking capability months ahead of predictions; the acceleration of long-horizon capability is faster than expected
actionable: Expect model capabilities to improve faster than published research predictions; stay flexible in long-term planning

claim: Long-horizon task capability matters more than traditional benchmarks (MMLU, ARC) which are saturating; extending autonomous task duration is the new frontier of AI progress
actionable: When evaluating AI progress, focus on long-horizon task metrics; traditional benchmarks are becoming less discriminative

claim: Claude Sonnet 4.5 is same price as Sonnet 4 ($3/M input, $15/M output); no price increase despite major capability jump
actionable: Upgrade to Sonnet 4.5 immediately; you get major performance gains without additional cost

claim: The vast majority of Claude's own code and next-generation design is now written by Claude; Anthropic uses its own models for development
actionable: This is a strong signal of model capability; use the same tools for your own development

claim: Claude Imagine demo shows real-time app generation with Claude building interactive apps (email client, calculator, to-do list) from text prompts on a virtual desktop
actionable: This preview shows the future of software development; prepare for on-demand, generative UI systems

claim: Anthropic hardcoded political facts (Donald Trump is president, inaugurated Jan 20 2025) in system prompt; firms are explicitly encoding controversial facts to prevent viral jailbreaks
actionable: Be aware that models have hardcoded facts to prevent controversial outputs; this is a common pattern across frontier labs
